titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xv.21

div.p: Malcolm, king of Scotland, to bishops, abbots, justices, earls, sheriffs, barons, officials, and all worthy men of his entire kingdom and of the Honour of Huntingdon, clerk and lay, greeting. Let those to come after as well as those present know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, Saint Mary, and the monks of the Cistercian Order who are at the house of Sartis, in everlasting alms for the construction of an abbey, for the salvation of my own soul and my predecessors' and successors' [souls], the whole of Sawtry, with all its pertinents in wood and plain, in meadows and waters, and pastures and marshes, and all other property, just as David the king, my grandfather of good memory, held it, and just as Henry, king of England, has now returned to me with my other rights of Huntingdon. Wherefore I will and firmly command that the aforesaid monks have and hold it of me and my heirs, just as my own alms; well, in peace, honourably, quitly, and free from all temporal exaction and service, just as other monks of the same order in my kingdom most freely and quitly have and hold their alms. With these as witnesses: Herbert, bishop of Glasgow; Waldef, abbot of Melrose; Arnold, abbot of Roxburgh; Osbert, abbot of Jedburgh; Walter the chancellor; Adam the chaplain of Roxburgh; Nicholas the clerk; William my brother; Walter fitz Alan; Richard de Morville; Ranulf de Soulis; William de Morville; William de Somerville; Liulf son of Maccus; Orm son of Eilaf; David Oliphant; Robert Frebern; David de Boeville; Henry de Percy. At Roxburgh.
